title: SQL Joins and Aggregation
summary: JOIN types (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL), GROUP BY, HAVING, aggregate functions, and subqueries.
content_html:
JOIN Types

JOINs combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column.

Tables: Students(ID, Name, DeptID)  Departments(DeptID, DeptName)

INNER JOIN (only matching rows):
SELECT s.Name, d.DeptName
FROM Students s INNER JOIN Departments d ON s.DeptID = d.DeptID;

LEFT JOIN (all from left, matching from right or NULL):
SELECT s.Name, d.DeptName
FROM Students s LEFT JOIN Departments d ON s.DeptID = d.DeptID;

RIGHT JOIN (all from right, matching from left or NULL):
SELECT s.Name, d.DeptName
FROM Students s RIGHT JOIN Departments d ON s.DeptID = d.DeptID;

FULL OUTER JOIN (all from both, NULL where no match):
SELECT s.Name, d.DeptName
FROM Students s FULL OUTER JOIN Departments d ON s.DeptID = d.DeptID;

Aggregate Functions

COUNT(*): number of rows
COUNT(col): number of non-NULL values in col
SUM(col): sum of values
AVG(col): average
MIN(col): minimum value
MAX(col): maximum value

Examples:
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Students;
SELECT AVG(Age), MAX(Age), MIN(Age) FROM Students;
SELECT SUM(Salary) FROM Employees WHERE Dept = 'Sales';

GROUP BY

Aggregate by groups:

SELECT Major, COUNT(*) AS NumStudents, AVG(Age)
FROM Students
GROUP BY Major;
-- Returns one row per Major with count and avg age

HAVING (Filter After GROUP BY)

SELECT Major, COUNT(*) AS NumStudents
FROM Students
GROUP BY Major
HAVING COUNT(*) > 10;   -- only majors with more than 10 students
-- WHERE filters rows BEFORE grouping
-- HAVING filters groups AFTER grouping

Subqueries

-- Students older than average age:
SELECT Name FROM Students
WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Students);

-- Students in departments with more than 5 members:
SELECT Name FROM Students
WHERE DeptID IN (
  SELECT DeptID FROM Students GROUP BY DeptID HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
);

Query Execution Order

FROM → JOIN → WHERE → GROUP BY → HAVING → SELECT → ORDER BY → LIMIT

exercises.title: INNER JOIN result
options: Only rows with matching values in both tables
All rows from the left table
exercises.answer: Only rows with matching values in both tables

exercises.title: LEFT JOIN unmatched
exercises.prompt: In a LEFT JOIN, rows from the left table with no match appear:
options: With NULL for right table columns
exercises.answer: With NULL for right table columns

exercises.title: COUNT(*) vs COUNT(col)
options: All rows ; only non-NULL values in col
exercises.answer: All rows ; only non-NULL values in col

exercises.title: GROUP BY purpose
options: Aggregate rows into groups by column value
exercises.answer: Aggregate rows into groups by column value

exercises.title: HAVING vs WHERE
options: Groups after GROUP BY ; rows before grouping
exercises.answer: Groups after GROUP BY ; rows before grouping

exercises.title: Subquery usage
exercises.prompt: A subquery in WHERE clause is used to:
options: Filter based on results of another query
exercises.answer: Filter based on results of another query

exercises.title: Query order
exercises.prompt: In SQL query execution, which clause is processed first?

exercises.title: FULL OUTER JOIN
options: All rows from both tables, NULL where no match
exercises.answer: All rows from both tables, NULL where no match

exercises.title: JOIN condition
exercises.prompt: The ON clause in a JOIN specifies:
options: The condition connecting rows from both tables
exercises.answer: The condition connecting rows from both tables
